title: melampyrum vaudense
intro: Melampyrum vaudense, also known as Vaud's melampyrum or Vaud's cow-wheat, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This hemiparasitic plant, which obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants, has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may provide health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving pain. The herbal preparations of Melampyrum vaudense are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in water or using them as a tincture, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
